text.title: Rails Service Object Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Worked examples of service implementation, controller wiring, and RSpec spec; plus antipatterns to reject.

example.code:
module Users class CreateService def initialize(params:, current_user: nil) @params = params @current_user = current_user end
def call user = User.new(user_params) ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do user.save! UserMailer.welcome(user).deliver_later AuditLog.create!( action: 'user.created', resource: user, actor: @current_user ) end ServiceResult.success(user) rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e ServiceResult.failure(e.record.errors.full_messages) end
private
def user_params @params.slice(:name, :email, :password) end end end
example.note: Service accepts plain hash, not ActionController::Parameters. Mailer uses deliver_later. Transaction wraps save + audit.

example.code:
module Api module V1 class UsersController < ApplicationController def create result = Users::CreateService.new( params: user_params, current_user: current_user ).call
if result.success? render json: UserSerializer.new(result.data), status: :created else render json: { errors: result.errors }, status: :unprocessable_entity end end
private
def user_params params.require(:user).permit(:name, :email, :password) end end end end
example.note: Controller only maps ServiceResult to HTTP shape. user_params is permitted before passing to service.

antipattern.description: Service has multiple public methods (call, create, update) or no call method at all.
antipattern.reason: Breaks single-responsibility; unclear which method is the entry point; harder to compose with organizers.

antipattern.description: rescue StandardError inside the service, swallowing all exceptions into ServiceResult.failure.
antipattern.reason: Hides real bugs (NoMethodError, TypeError) as domain failures. Only catch documented domain exceptions.

antipattern.description: Service returns raw ActiveRecord object on success, exception on failure — two divergent paths.
antipattern.reason: Controllers must handle both paths differently; ServiceResult enforces a uniform interface.

text.reference: RSpec spec template covering success, validation failure, and DB rollback
